# dear-diary

> Vim-first terminal diary app for macOS. Opens daily Markdown journal entries in Vim, provides a Bubble Tea TUI calendar browser, and searches local journal history from the command line.

## Canonical repository

https://github.com/borankux/dear-diary

## Summary

dear-diary is a local-first command-line journaling tool written in Go. The default command, `diary`, opens today's Markdown journal file under `~/Documents/dear-diary/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. Reopening today's entry appends a new timestamp section instead of overwriting previous writing. `diary browse` opens a terminal calendar view, and `diary search <keyword>` searches all journal entries.

## Primary audience

- Developers who journal from the terminal
- Vim or Neovim users
- People who prefer local Markdown files over hosted journal services
- Users building a long-term personal archive that can be backed up with normal file tools

## Key commands

- `diary`: open today's journal entry
- `diary browse`: browse entries in a TUI calendar
- `diary today`: open today explicitly
- `diary yesterday` or `diary y`: open yesterday
- `diary <date>`: open a specific date
- `diary search <keyword>`: search local journal entries

## Important files

- `README.md`: user-facing overview, installation, usage, and FAQ
- `docs/spec.md`: product and implementation spec
- `cmd/diary/main.go`: CLI command dispatch
- `internal/storage/storage.go`: Markdown storage model
- `internal/search/search.go`: search implementation
- `internal/tui/`: Bubble Tea calendar and search result UI

## Positioning

dear-diary is not a hosted journaling platform, database-backed note app, or rich-text editor. It is a small local-first tool that routes dates, opens Vim, keeps Markdown files organized, and adds terminal browsing and search.
